commit.message:
Fix: Enable Supabase environment variable configuration for Vercel deployment

- Add environment variable initialization in index.html (window.SUPABASE_URL and window.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)
- Update supabase-storage.js to read environment variables from window object
- This allows Vercel to inject credentials at runtime without hardcoding secrets
- Maintains fallback to placeholder values for local development

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix author audit bypass: thread effectivePass through FetchFromProviderAsync

HarvestRequest.Pass defaults to HydrationPass.Quick. With two_pass_enabled=false,
the pipeline correctly computed effectivePass=Universe but then discarded it when
building the ProviderLookupRequest — passing request.Pass (Quick) instead.

WikidataAdapter gates the author audit (pen name detection, ordinal sorting,
collective member expansion) on HydrationPass.Universe, so the audit never ran
even in single-pass mode. Adding effectivePass parameter to FetchFromProviderAsync
and BuildLookupRequest ensures the resolved pass is used, not the request default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix 5 bugs found by Codex analysis

1. Date timezone: new parseLocalDate() for "YYYY-MM-DD" strings to avoid
   UTC interpretation showing wrong day in French timezone
2. Decimal comma: new parseNum() handles French "0,95" input for IPS
   grading and BMI calculation (was silently returning 0)
3. Dead code: remove unused basCollants field from consult model and
   copy-forward list (no UI ever used it)
4. Memory leak: revoke object URL after export download
5. Service worker: pre-cache core assets on install for true offline
   first-load support, skip registration in dev mode, only cache
   successful responses, bump cache version to v3
6. SpeechRecognition: guard window access for SSR/test safety

commit.message:
Fix dynamic rendering: use cookies() instead of unstable_noStore in getSession

unstable_noStore() throws DynamicServerError from inside an async helper,
which propagates as an unhandled 500 before Next.js can catch and recover it.

cookies() from next/headers is the primary dynamic API that Next.js's static
renderer has special catch logic for — calling it in getSession() immediately
signals the route as dynamic and prevents the transient 500 on first load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace force-dynamic with unstable_noStore() to fix dev rendering error

force-dynamic works by throwing an internal DynamicServerError at module
load time. In Next.js 14 dev mode this error escapes the framework's own
catch handler and surfaces as a visible page error.

unstable_noStore() achieves the same result (opts the route out of static
generation for both dev and production builds) but fires at call-time
during the render, which the framework handles correctly.

One call in getSession() covers every page that needs auth — no per-page
declarations required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement Supabase migration: cloud sync and cross-device data sharing

- Add js/supabase-storage.js: ES module storage layer with Supabase + IndexedDB fallback
  * Maintains identical window.appStorage API for zero-change to business logic
  * Pre-fetches all tables on startup (warm-cache pattern)
  * Lazy-loads transaction data by year with 5K-row chunked upserts
  * Auto-detects local data and triggers migration flow

- Update login.html: Replace local auth with Supabase Auth
  * signInWithPassword() with email format: {username}@dk.internal
  * Queries profiles table for user details
  * Maintains sessionStorage format for compatibility

- Update index.html: Switch storage module to supabase-storage.js (type="module")

- Modify js/app.js: Three surgical changes
  * Line ~3690: Add supabase.auth.signOut() to logout handler
  * Line ~2366: Add lazy-load call for transactions on year change
  * Line ~4401: Show migration banner if local data detected

- Update js/admin-features.js: Restrict new user creation
  * New users must be created via Supabase dashboard (no service-key access)
  * Show guidance message instead of local creation

- Add SUPABASE_SETUP.md: Comprehensive setup guide
  * Full SQL schema with 7 tables and RLS policies
  * Step-by-step configuration instructions
  * Initial user creation walkthrough

Users can now access dashboard from any PC with synchronized data via Supabase.
Local data auto-migrates on first login via banner prompt.

commit.message:
fix: reduce num_recycling 3→1 and num_samples 4→1 to fit A100-80GB

N=112 at recycling=3 OOMs at ~70 GiB even with block_remat=True.
Reducing recycling to 1 cuts gradient memory ~3x to ~23 GiB.
Reducing InverseFoldingSequenceRecovery num_samples from 4 to 1
removes 3 extra ProteinMPNN gradient passes per step.

commit.message:
Add force-dynamic to server component pages that call getSession()

getSession() calls getServerSession() which reads HTTP cookies — this
makes any server component that calls it dynamic. Adding force-dynamic
to each affected page tells Next.js not to attempt static pre-rendering
at build time, resolving the dynamic server usage error.

Only server components are affected; client components are unchanged.

Update CLAUDE.md with Plus Jakarta Sans font reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix root cause of force-dynamic error: remove getServerSession from layout

The root layout was calling getServerSession() on every request, making
the entire app dynamic at build time and causing Railway to reject the build.

Navbar and BottomTabBar are client components that already use useSession()
from next-auth/react — they never needed the server session passed down.
SessionProvider works without an initial session; it fetches it client-side.

The layout is now a plain static component. Protected pages still call
getSession() individually and redirect unauthenticated users.

fix: disable onlyPublishWithReleaseLabel for alpha pre-releases

auto's onlyPublishWithReleaseLabel blocks publishing even on prerelease
branches unless the PR has a 'release' label. Disabling it lets auto
publish on any merge with a semver label (patch/minor/major).

The release workflow's `if` condition still gates stable releases on
master to require the 'release' label — auto just handles the version
bump, the workflow controls when it runs.

commit.message:
fix: reduce num_recycling 10→3 to avoid OOM in gradient optimization

Backprop through 10 recycling steps requires storing all intermediate
activations, which needs ~49 GiB at N=57 (smoke test) and ~190 GiB at
N=112 (full design) — both infeasible. num_recycling=3 reduces memory
to ~5-8 GiB at N=57 and ~57 GiB at N=112, fitting on A100-80GB.

commit.message:
Fix dynamic rendering at root: add force-dynamic to app/layout.tsx

Root cause: layout.tsx calls getServerSession() (reads cookies/headers)
without declaring itself dynamic. This caused all pages to fail static
generation regardless of per-page force-dynamic declarations.

Declaring force-dynamic on the root layout propagates to all child pages,
removing the need for the redundant per-page declarations.

commit.message:
fix(sku-parser): remover fallback por prefixo e corrigir validações

Remove mecanismo de fallback por prefixo de modelo que não estava na spec
(TASK-4) e causava parse incorreto: SKU 61001232 era atribuído ao modelo
61001 (prefixo mais longo) em vez de 610/012/32 pelo modo SUFIXO.

- Remove resolverPorPrefixoModelo e toda lógica de itensPendentes/fallbackResults
- Corrige critério RESOLVIDO em modo SEPARADOR: !!modelo && !!cor && !!tamanho
  (antes dependia de regra.ordem.every, quebrando com campos extras)
- Corrige validação de tamanho: regex /^\d+$/ em vez de parseInt permissivo
  (antes aceitava "32A" como 32 silenciosamente)

Daily product news update - 2026-03-16

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: NoCys must wrap AF3Loss, not inner loss combination

NoCys expects a 19-dim PSSM and converts to 20-dim before calling its
inner loss. If NoCys wraps only the inner_loss combination (not AF3Loss),
AF3Loss.__call__ receives the raw 19-dim PSSM and passes it directly to
set_binder_sequence, which pads to profile_dim-20 dims → shape mismatch.

Fix: NoCys(loss=model.build_loss(loss=inner_loss, ...))
so the call chain is: optimizer(19-dim) → NoCys → 20-dim → AF3Loss → losses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
